meta.title: UK Window Tax material ledger

rightsStatuses.confirmed: Reuse is supported by an item-level or collection-level rights statement and the required conditions are recorded.
rightsStatuses.conditional: Reuse is permitted only while stated limits or a final asset-specific check are satisfied.
rightsStatuses.permission-needed: Do not publish the asset until written permission or another sufficient licence is recorded.
rightsStatuses.link-only: Link to the item page but do not copy the material.
rightsStatuses.rights-conflict: Two current or historical records conflict and the grant chain has not been established.
rightsStatuses.unknown: No sufficient reuse statement was confirmed.

meta.publicationRule: A localPath must remain null until rights are confirmed or every recorded condition is satisfied.

materials.title: A vision of the repeal of the window-tax

source.creator: Richard Doyle
source.locator: Punch, volume 18, January-June 1850, page 165; H 634-3 Folio

evidence.description: A family in a dark room welcomes an anthropomorphic sun in a visual argument for repeal.
supports: A contemporary satirical representation of repeal as the return of daylight.
The cultural and political framing of the duty as a tax on light.
doesNotProve: The prevalence of dark housing.
A quantified health effect.
That the depicted family is a real household.

licence.name: Public Domain Mark 1.0; catalogue also records CC0 1.0

rights.creditLine: Punch, 18.1850, January to June, 1850, p. 165. Universitätsbibliothek Heidelberg.
conditions: Keep the item-page link and library credit.
Record any crop, rotation, compression or tonal adjustment.

accessibility.requiredAlternative: Describe the dark room, family and personified sun, and identify the work as satire.

materials.title: Dumfriesshire Window Tax roll showing windows closed since the previous survey

source.publisher: National Records of Scotland via Scotland's People
source.creator: Tax-roll clerk; exact name not stated

evidence.description: The published extract and transcription contain entries recording one, three and five windows closed since the previous survey.
supports: Specific assessed properties had fewer open windows than at the previous survey.
Window closure was recorded by the tax administration.
doesNotProve: Why every listed opening was closed.
That closure was common across all households.
That every modern blocked window was caused by the tax.

rights.creditLine: Crown copyright, National Records of Scotland, E326/1/32, page 73.
conditions: Count this within the maximum of 20 NRS Crown-copyright digital images used online per website.
Reproduce accurately.
Do not use in a misleading context.
Acknowledge NRS.
Resolve and record the exact source image URL at acquisition time.

accessibility.requiredAlternative: Describe the relevant rows and the handwritten closed-window notes without requiring the reader to decipher the scan.

source.publisher: National Records of Scotland via Scotland's People
source.creator: Tax-roll clerk; exact name not stated

evidence.description: The archive feature reports 294 windows surveyed at Floors Castle.
supports: A high-window-count Scottish assessment in 1748.
How the visible proxy operated at the top end of the property scale.
doesNotProve: How representative the castle was.
That the tax was proportionate across household types.
The owner's subjective view of the assessment.

rights.creditLine: Crown copyright, National Records of Scotland, E326/1/111, page 4.
conditions: Count this within the maximum of 20 NRS Crown-copyright digital images used online per website.
Reproduce accurately and in a non-misleading context.
Acknowledge NRS.

accessibility.requiredAlternative: State the record identity and the 294-window figure; do not require handwriting recognition.

source.publisher: National Records of Scotland via Scotland's People
source.creator: Tax-roll clerk; exact name not stated

evidence.description: A handwriting exercise built from the first Window Tax roll for Crail.
supports: A real Scottish roll and the practical form of the record.
An accessible read-the-record interaction when paired with a transcription.
doesNotProve: National representativeness.
Why a particular resident had a particular number of windows.

rights.creditLine: Crown copyright, National Records of Scotland, E326/1/42, page 19.
conditions: Count this within the maximum of 20 NRS Crown-copyright digital images used online per website.
Reproduce accurately and in a non-misleading context.
Acknowledge NRS.

accessibility.requiredAlternative: Explain the page structure and provide the relevant transcription beside it.

source.publisher: Geograph Britain and Ireland via Wikimedia Commons
source.creator: Stephen Richards

evidence.description: A modern photograph of the facade. Historic England says the ground-floor windows were paired to reduce the facade count from 14 to 12, presumably to reduce the Window Tax.
supports: The visible present facade.
An official qualified interpretation of a probable tax-related alteration.
doesNotProve: The owner's recorded intention.
When the alteration was made without further evidence.
That similar alterations on other buildings had the same cause.

rights.creditLine: 3 Edgar Street, Worcester by Stephen Richards, CC BY-SA 2.0.
conditions: Link the creator, source and licence.
Indicate changes.
Distribute adaptations under the same or a compatible licence.

accessibility.requiredAlternative: Describe the paired ground-floor windows relevant to the 14-to-12 interpretation, without asserting certain motive.

materials.title: The National Archives Window Tax transcript

evidence.description: A short archive explainer connecting the tax to clipped coinage and the 1755 receipt.
supports: The archive's interpretation of the tax's origin and later repeal.
An accessible text alternative for its narration.
doesNotProve: That the interpretation replaces the enacted statute.
Rights to republish the receipt image.

rights.creditLine: Contains public sector information licensed under the Open Government Licence v3.0. Source: The National Archives.
conditions: Keep source attribution and link.
Do not imply that the text licence clears the collection record image.

accessibility.requiredAlternative: The transcript is itself the accessible alternative.

materials.title: The National Archives Window Tax narration

source.creator: The National Archives
source.locator: Audio embedded in the Window Tax education resource

evidence.description: An audio version of the archive's short Window Tax account.
supports: An optional listening route for the archive's interpretation.
doesNotProve: Rights to republish the receipt image.
A stronger historical claim than the transcript supports.

licence.name: Open Government Licence v3.0, subject to a final audio-specific notice check

rights.creditLine: Contains public sector information licensed under the Open Government Licence v3.0. Source: The National Archives.
conditions: Confirm that the MP3 carries no separate third-party notice immediately before integration.
Use native controls and no autoplay.
Publish the complete transcript beside the audio.

accessibility.requiredAlternative: Provide the complete written transcript and do not autoplay.

evidence.description: A genuine receipt for payment of Window Tax.
supports: The practical existence and form of a 1755 tax receipt.
doesNotProve: Typical liability or payment across the population.
Permission to republish the image merely because the page is public.

conditions: Obtain Image Library permission before online publication.
Record the permission scope, duration, territory, credit and any fee.

accessibility.requiredAlternative: Provide a transcription and explain the fields relevant to the story.

materials.title: St Paul, Covent Garden Window Tax petition report

evidence.description: Hansard reports a repeal petition signed by 400 inhabitants of the parish.
supports: A documented petition report and public opposition route.
doesNotProve: That the signatures represented the whole population.
The appearance of the original petition document.
That every statement in the reported speech was correct.

rights.creditLine: Contains Parliamentary information licensed under the Open Parliament Licence v3.0.
conditions: Keep the attribution and source link.
Identify the material as Hansard archive text, which may contain typographical errors.
Do not present styled text as a facsimile of the original petition.

accessibility.requiredAlternative: Render the selected text as real HTML, not text embedded in an image.

materials.title: St James, Bath petition for unconditional repeal

evidence.description: Hansard reports a Bath petition seeking unconditional repeal without a substitute tax, and records why the petition was ruled informal.
supports: The content of a reported petition and the Parliamentary receiving rule discussed that day.
doesNotProve: That Parliament accepted the petition.
The appearance of the original petition.
That unconditional repeal was representative national opinion.

rights.creditLine: Contains Parliamentary information licensed under the Open Parliament Licence v3.0.
conditions: Keep the attribution and source link.
Identify the material as Hansard archive text, which may contain typographical errors.
Do not present styled text as a facsimile of the original petition.

accessibility.requiredAlternative: Render the selected text as real HTML, not text embedded in an image.

evidence.description: A catalogue lead for a measured-drawings collection item concerning the 1770 Window Tax.
supports: The existence of a potentially useful archive map.
doesNotProve: The map's detailed content before inspection.
Reuse rights.
That the map is necessary for the first story.

rights.creditLine: To be supplied by Historic England Archive.
conditions: Request a reproduction licence and price before publication.
Record creator, date, rights holder, permitted uses and exact credit.

accessibility.requiredAlternative: If acquired, provide a long description of the map's relevant labels, boundaries and meaning.

materials.title: Wellcome glass-plate reproduction of A vision of the repeal of the Window Tax

source.creator: 1952 reproduction of an illustration from Punch

evidence.description: A glass-plate photographic reproduction of the same Punch illustration available directly from Heidelberg.
supports: The existence of Wellcome's 1952 reproduction.
doesNotProve: A clear current reuse grant.
Any historical claim stronger than the original Punch page.

conditions: The current Wellcome record says in copyright.
An older Wikimedia Commons record says CC BY 4.0.
Do not rely on the older record without establishing the licence grant chain.
Use the Heidelberg public-domain scan instead.

accessibility.requiredAlternative: Not applicable while publication is blocked.

source.locator: Exact archive reference not exposed in accessible page markup

evidence.description: The page says a February 1850 Sunderland Corporation entry is illustrated, but does not expose sufficient item provenance or rights.
supports: A research lead and the page's official historical interpretation.
doesNotProve: The exact identity or rights status of the displayed illustration.
Permission to copy a Parliamentary Archives image.

rights.creditLine: Link to the Parliament page only.
conditions: Do not copy the illustration.
Parliamentary Archives images are excluded from the Open Parliament Licence.
Seek an item reference and separate permission if the image becomes necessary.

accessibility.requiredAlternative: Use the page's sourced text summary unless the original item is identified and cleared.

materials.title: Blank windows at Portland Street, Southampton

evidence.description: A modern photograph of blank windows in a terrace built around 1830.
supports: The visible blank-window arrangement.
The building identity and date when paired with the official list entry.
doesNotProve: That Window Tax caused the blank windows.
That the uploader's causal caption is independently verified.

conditions: Link the creator, source and licence.
Indicate changes.
Distribute adaptations under the same or a compatible licence.
Do not repeat the causal caption as fact.

accessibility.requiredAlternative: Describe the visible blank openings and state that the image alone cannot establish why they are blank.
